$comment: Shadow Document Source-Map v1. The parser-agnostic binding between a raw source document and the structured values extracted from it: every extracted value carries the page + region + verbatim raw_text it came from, and the whole map is bound to a hash of the raw file. This is the moat piece the OSS-radar (2026-07-20) identified — NOT another parser. The parser (OpenDataLoader PDF, MarkItDown, OfficeCLI, an OCR service) is a swappable backend recorded in `parser`/`parser_version`; Shadow owns the verifiable binding conclusion -> page -> region -> extracted value -> audit event. Discipline: `confidence` is the parser's self-report and is NEVER evidence of correctness — the map records what the parser CLAIMED plus the raw region, so a human or a second parser can verify. document_hash makes the map post-hoc-swap-evident (same pattern as reason_code_dictionary_sha256 governance): swap the underlying file after the fact and verifyBoundToDocument() fails. source_map_hash lets the map be pinned as an attestation-bound evidence field, exactly like dictionary_hash.
v1_1_note: 1.1.0 adds the OPTIONAL render_hash / render_media_type — bind the human-visible rendering of the document, not just its raw bytes. Surfaced by the office-evidence spike: hidden columns, a formula vs its cached value, or a chart can diverge between 'what the parser extracted' and 'what a reviewer saw', so evidence should be able to bind both. Back-compat: 1.0.0 maps stay valid; the fields are optional.
binds: one raw source document (PDF / DOCX / XLSX / PPTX / image) to its structured extractions

top_level.document_hash: sha256:<hex> of the RAW source bytes — binds this map to that exact file
top_level.render_hash: OPTIONAL (v1.1) sha256:<hex> of the human-visible RENDERED form (e.g. the page/sheet PNG) — binds what a reviewer saw, catching hidden/derived content the raw value drops
top_level.render_media_type: OPTIONAL (v1.1) media type of the render, e.g. image/png
top_level.parser: backend id, e.g. opendataloader-pdf | markitdown | officecli | claude-vision
top_level.parser_version: backend version string — part of reproducibility, not correctness
top_level.source_map_hash: OPTIONAL sha256:<hex> over the map excluding this field itself — pin it as an attestation-bound evidence field

entry.field: OPTIONAL id of the normalized field / claim this entry supports (e.g. debt_to_income, or a claim id)
entry.page: 1-based page (or sheet index for spreadsheets); positive integer
entry.region: OPTIONAL [x0,y0,x1,y1] normalized to 0..1 (page-level if absent); x0<=x1, y0<=y1
entry.content_type: one of: line | paragraph | table_cell | table | figure | formula | image | heading | other
entry.raw_text: verbatim text the parser read at this region — the ground the value is derived from
entry.normalized_value: OPTIONAL typed value the parser derived (number / string / bool)
entry.confidence: OPTIONAL parser self-reported 0..1; NOT correctness — never gate a decision on it alone

checker: lib/document-source-map.js — validateSourceMap (structural), computeDocumentHash (bind), verifyBoundToDocument (post-hoc-swap detection), computeSourceMapHash (attestation pin)
